summary: AI security platform for agent red teaming, runtime defense, and adversarial evaluation.
editor_note: The bet is that AI security needs an adversarial operating loop, not a static checklist. Gray Swan combines Cygnal runtime protection, Shade automated red teaming, and Arena's 15,000-person attack network into one platform. Unlike compliance-first AI safety tools, its evidence comes from frontier-lab evaluations and real attack trajectories. The risk is whether enterprise teams can turn findings into production controls quickly.
tags: ai security,red teaming,runtime protection,model evaluation,agent security

product_evidence.claim: Gray Swan's platform is described as Cygnal for real-time AI monitoring and protection, Shade for automated adversarial testing, and Arena for a global red-team community.
The funding post says Arena includes more than 15,000 researchers and over one million real-world attack trajectories used to train Cygnal and Shade.

market_context.primary_user: Frontier labs and enterprise security teams evaluating and protecting AI models, agents, and applications.
market_context.differentiation: Security product telemetry fed by adversarial research, frontier-lab evaluations, and live red-team attacks.
market_context.why_now: The May 28, 2026 Series A gives Gray Swan funding to scale enterprise AI security as agents move into production.

risks.claim: The execution risk is whether enterprises can operationalize adversarial findings faster than AI applications expand.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product and funding-source review.
